She rose to fame in 2015 as the first runner-up on Pinoy Big Brother: 737 at age 13, launching a music and acting career in the Philippines with Star Music. Her debut EP "My Name Is Ylona Garcia" (2016) was certified Gold. She later transitioned internationally, signing with 88rising's sub-label Paradise Rising in 2021, releasing singles like "All That," "Don't Go Changing," and the VALORANT-collaboration "Entertain Me" (2022). She became the first Filipino featured in V Magazine (2022), relocated to Los Angeles, and joined gaming brand 100 Thieves as a content creator in 2024. Her musical style spans R&B, pop, and soft rock, and she continues to release music internationally with her latest singles "Question Mark" and "Could've Been" arriving in January 2026.

## Biography of Ylona Garcia

**Ylona Jade Garcia** (born February 28, 2002) is a Filipino-Australian singer, songwriter, actress, and content creator. She is known for her genre-blending R&B and pop music, her breakthrough on Philippine reality television, and her international career with the label 88rising and gaming brand 100 Thieves.

### Early Life

Ylona Jade Garcia was born on February 28, 2002, in Sydney, Australia. She is the third of five siblings. Her father works as a pathology technician, and her mother is a nurse and medical technologist. [Г¦ВќВҐГ¦ВєВђ: Wikipedia ГўВЂВ“ Ylona Garcia ГўВ†В’ Early life]

### Career Breakthrough: Pinoy Big Brother (2015)

In 2015, at just 13 years old, Garcia auditioned for ABS-CBN's reality television series **Pinoy Big Brother: 737**. She was selected after showcasing her singing, acting, and dancing abilities, as well as her "confidence and candid remarks." During her time in the Big Brother house, she composed and recorded two original songs: **"Win the Fight"** (co-written with fellow housemate Jimboy Martin) and **"Magmahal Muli"** (recorded with Bailey May). She finished as the **first runner-up** of the teen edition, receiving 42.24% of the public vote. [Г¦ВќВҐГ¦ВєВђ: Wikipedia ГўВЂВ“ Ylona Garcia ГўВ†В’ 2015ГўВЂВ“2020]

### Music and Acting Career in the Philippines (2015ГўВЂВ“2020)

After Pinoy Big Brother, Garcia's career rapidly expanded:

- **Acting**: She was cast as **Audrey Olivar** in ABS-CBN's romantic comedy series *On the Wings of Love* (2015ГўВЂВ“2016) alongside Bailey May. She also appeared in *Sana Dalawa ang Puso* (2018) and the PBB film *Trending: Love* (2015).
- **Music**: She was signed by **Star Music** in February 2016. Her debut extended play, **"My Name Is Ylona Garcia"**, was released on August 5, 2016, and was certified **Gold in the Philippines**. Notable singles from this era include "Dahan Dahan Dahan Lang," "Fly Tonight," and "O Pag-Ibig" (with Bailey May).
- **Television**: She joined the reality music competition *We Love OPM* as part of Team Yeng: Oh My Girls (finishing as runners-up). She was also a Celebrity VJ for Myx and a performer on the variety show *ASAP*, where she was part of the groups ASAP BFF5 and ASAP G!.
- In **2019**, she transferred from Star Music to **Homeworkz** for her recording label.
- She released several singles in 2019ГўВЂВ“2020 including "Maybe a Little Bit" and "Walk In My Timbs" (featuring Jay-R), showcasing her songwriting abilities.

### International Career with 88rising (2021ГўВЂВ“Present)

In February 2021, Garcia was officially signed to **88rising**, an Asian American-oriented record label, through its sub-label **Paradise Rising** (a joint venture with Globe Telecom focused on Filipino artists). This marked a pivotal shift in her career toward international music markets. [Г¦ВќВҐГ¦ВєВђ: Wikipedia ГўВЂВ“ Ylona Garcia ГўВ†В’ 2021ГўВЂВ“present]

Key milestones include:

- **"All That"** (March 2021) ГўВЂВ“ Her debut single with 88rising, whose music video garnered over 1 million YouTube views in four days.
- **"Don't Go Changing"** (August 2021) ГўВЂВ“ Released as part of the *Head in the Clouds* album.
- **Head in the Clouds Festival 2021** ГўВЂВ“ She performed a 20-minute set at the Rose Bowl Stadium in Pasadena, California.
- **"Entertain Me"** (January 2022) ГўВЂВ“ Released in partnership with the tactical shooter game **VALORANT** by Riot Games. The song was featured in the trailer for the game's Filipino Agent, **Neon**. It charted at #35 on the New Zealand Hot chart.
- In **January 2022**, she announced she had relocated to **Los Angeles, California**, to pursue her music career full-time.
- In **August 2022**, she became the **first Filipino** to be featured in American fashion magazine **V**, which called her "the new global voice leading R&B Pop."
- In **August 2024**, she joined **100 Thieves**, a gaming and lifestyle brand, as a content creator.
- **"Sick of It"** (February 2025) ГўВЂВ“ A soft rock single she wrote about growing up in a "toxic" entertainment industry.
- **"Question Mark" and "Could've Been"** (January 2026) ГўВЂВ“ Her most recent single releases.

### Collaborations

Notably, in 2024, Garcia collaborated with popular streamers **Valkyrae** and **Fuslie** on the single **"Echoes,"** bridging the music and gaming worlds.

### Public Image

In June 2025, the Philippine Daily Inquirer described Garcia as having "long been more than just a pop star," calling her a "style icon" and "voice of her generation."

### Endorsements

Throughout her career, Garcia has endorsed major brands including Coca-Cola Philippines, Huawei, Jollibee, Bench, Pond's, Sunsilk, Mountain Dew, Fujifilm Instax, Clean & Clear, and more.
